title: narcissus juncifolius subsp. rupicola
intro: Narcissus juncifolius subsp. rupicola, also known as rock broom narcissus or mountain daffodil, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its narrow, grass-like leaves and yellow to white trumpet-shaped flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its potential anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Narcissus juncifolius subsp. rupicola are attributed to its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant's bulbs and flowers are often used to make infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
